message:
Use structured error codes for attachment manifest failures

Replace brittle string matching on error.message with stable error
codes (ATTACHMENT_REQUEST_INVALIDATED, ATTACHMENT_MANIFEST_ABSENT,
ATTACHMENT_MANIFEST_FETCH_FAILED, ATTACHMENT_MANIFEST_INVALID) via a
createAttachmentError helper. Critically, only cache manifest absence
on true 404s — transient fetch failures and parse errors no longer
permanently suppress attachment loading for the rest of the session.

Add shouldCacheManifestAbsence guard so isManifestLoaded is only set
when the manifest genuinely does not exist, allowing retries on
network or server errors.

Tests verify the new caching semantics, error code distinctions,
and stable code-based invalidation checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
